news.text: As millions lose weight on GLP-1s, they're starting to turn to retailers to refresh their wardrobes
Olympic skier Lindsey Vonn doesn't rule out the 2030 games: 'I would only do it if I could be fast'
Exclusive: Iran war doubles Russia's main oil revenue to $9 bln in April, Reuters calculations show - Reuters
Italy says EU should consider pausing budget rules if Iran crisis persists - Reuters
Israeli military says it has killed nephew of Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem - Reuters
China hopes 'relevant parties' can grasp chance at peace in Iran war - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Futures are ripping higher with the S&P 500 up 2.55% to $676, led by industrials (+3.75%) and tech (+3.10%). VIX is collapsing 9.59% to 31.66, signaling some relief in risk appetite despite ongoing Middle East tensions. This looks like a technical bounce after recent selling pressure, but geopolitical headlines remain the wildcard.

◆ What to Watch

  • Energy Sector Divergence: XLE down 3.51% despite oil revenue headlines - watch for continued pressure on energy names
  • GLP-1 Retail Play: Consumer discretionary up 2.83% on weight-loss wardrobe refresh story - retail names could see follow-through
  • Iran War Impact: Monitor geopolitical headlines for market direction changes - any escalation could reverse this bounce quickly
  • Light Earnings Day: WDFC and WAFD the only notable reports - focus stays on macro themes
✦ Bull Case
VIX collapse below 32 suggests oversold bounce has legs. Industrials leading could signal infrastructure/defense spending optimism amid geopolitical tensions.

◆ Sector Rotation

Clear risk-on rotation with cyclicals leading - industrials, materials, and tech all up 3%+. Energy is the lone laggard at -3.51%, likely profit-taking after recent oil price spikes. Defensive sectors (utilities, staples) lagging but still positive suggests this is genuine risk appetite, not just short covering.
⚡ Key Driver
Market torn between Middle East war premium (bullish oil/defense) and recession fears (bearish growth). Today's bounce suggests growth concerns temporarily winning.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $680: Key resistance from March highs - break above opens door to $690+
  • QQQ $610: Critical level that's held multiple times - failure here negates tech strength
  • VIX 30: Break below signals real fear subsiding, could fuel more upside
  • XLE $85: Energy support level - break below accelerates sector rotation away from oil
⚠ Bear Case
This bounce comes on light volume pre-market with no major catalysts. Any fresh Middle East headlines or European market weakness could quickly reverse gains.

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
